Volunteer.title: Volunteer
Volunteer.description: Represents a volunteer in the HR section of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Volunteer entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the volunteer.

lastName.description: The last name of the volunteer.

email.description: The email address of the volunteer.

phone.description: The phone number of the volunteer.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the volunteer.

skills.description: List of skills the volunteer possesses.

Setting.title: Setting
Setting.description: Represents a user-configurable setting for the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Setting entity.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The email address of the user.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user is an administrator.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Logo entity.

fileName.description: Name of the logo image file.

fileType.description: Type of the logo image file (e.g., PNG, JPEG).

fileSize.description: Size of the logo image file in bytes.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the logo was uploaded.

dataURL.description: Data URL representing the logo image.

SoldItemReceipt.title: SoldItemReceipt
SoldItemReceipt.description: Represents a receipt for sold items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SoldItemReceipt entity.

receiptNumber.description: The receipt number for the sold item.

itemCategories.description: List of item categories on the receipt.

Overtime.title: Overtime
Overtime.description: Represents an overtime record for an employee.

Bonus.title: Bonus
Bonus.description: Represents a loading/unloading bonus for an employee.

CashWithdrawal.title: CashWithdrawal
CashWithdrawal.description: Represents a cash withdrawal by an employee.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user (or an admin) can access their profile data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores user-specific settings. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user (or an admin) can access their settings data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique ID of the setting.

definition.description: Stores user-specific logos. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user (or an admin) can access their logo data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: logoId
params.description: The unique ID of the logo.

definition.description: Stores volunteer information. Admin roles will be enforced through a separate mechanism (e.g., a dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection).

params.name: volunteerId
params.description: The unique ID of the volunteer.

definition.description: Documents in this collection grant admin privileges to the corresponding user ID. Existence, not content, determines admin status.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores sold item receipts. Assumes that all authenticated users can create and view receipts.

params.name: receiptId
params.description: The unique ID of the receipt.

params.name: overtimeId
params.description: The unique ID of the overtime record.

params.name: bonusId
params.description: The unique ID of the bonus record.

params.name: withdrawalId
params.description: The unique ID of the withdrawal record.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to address the 'auth/configuration-not-found' error, which typically indicates an issue with Firebase initialization or configuration. However, based on the provided app description and entities, the structure prioritizes secure data management and adheres to the principle of Authorization Independence.  User settings and logos are stored as user-owned data. This approach enables simple security rules based on `request.auth.uid`. Volunteers and receipts are stored at the top level with the expectation that they are managed by admins or any authenticated user respectively. This structure relies on path-based ownership and simplifies security rules. All list operations are secured because data requiring different access roles is segregated into different collections.
